text.title: rag-failure-taxonomy Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of rag-failure-taxonomy enforces.

rule.statement: Use only the closed label set: retrieval-fail, generation-fail, chunk-boundary, stale-doc, wrong-doc-ranked, prompt-leak.
rule.rationale: Open-ended labels diverge between reviewers; closed sets stay diff-able.

rule.statement: Every label requires a short evidence quote (≤200 chars) from the answer or retrieved context.
rule.rationale: Labels without evidence can't be audited later.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one primary label; optional secondary labels allowed but limited to 1.
rule.rationale: Multi-label fanout makes dashboard counts double-count.

rule.statement: If two reviewers disagree, the case escalates to a third reviewer; final label is recorded with the reviewer ids.
rule.rationale: Disagreement is the signal that the taxonomy needs sharpening.

rule.statement: Revisit the taxonomy each quarter; add labels only with team consensus.
rule.rationale: Taxonomy without revisit decays into legacy.

rule.statement: Skip when there is no team-shared review process.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
